text.title: Serverless IaC and Templates Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the serverless-iac-and-templates methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: IaC tool MUST be chosen with explicit rationale: SAM for AWS-only + minimal abstraction; CDK for typed AWS + IDE help; Serverless Framework for multi-cloud + plugins; Pulumi / Terraform for org-wide multi-resource estates. Resume-driven picks MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Tool selection has multi-year operational cost; switching costs 2-4 quarters.

rule.statement: Templates MUST be parameterised by stage (dev / staging / prod); per-stage overrides MUST live in a config file (samconfig.toml, terraform.tfvars, cdk.json), NOT as conditional branches inside CI scripts.
rule.rationale: Per-stage divergence is the dominant source of 'works in dev, breaks in prod' incidents; config files are the auditable shape.

rule.statement: Function IAM roles MUST follow least privilege: action × resource scoping per function; wildcard `*` resources MUST be flagged in CI; account-wide admin roles MUST NOT be used for deploy.

rationale.source: AWS IAM best practices; serverless breach case studies.

rule.rationale: Over-permissive IAM is the dominant compromise path in serverless breaches; least privilege caps blast radius.

rule.statement: Deployment pipeline MUST run drift detection (CloudFormation detect-stack-drift, Terraform plan in read-only mode) after deploy and alert when drift is detected.
rule.rationale: Console-edit drift creeps in regardless of policy; mechanical detection catches it within hours, not months.

rule.statement: Templates MUST be validated (sam validate / cdk synth / tflint / cfn-lint) AND statically scanned (cfn-nag, tfsec, checkov) in CI before deploy.
rule.rationale: Static checks catch IAM wildcards, missing encryption, public access defaults — issues hard to spot in review.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (inventory, account, environments, standard tool), apply r1 → r5 and emit the config + pipeline.
rule.rationale: Order encodes risk: tool first, parameterisation second, IAM third, drift fourth, static checks last.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) throwaway experiment, (b) single function with existing pattern, (c) migration to containers planned. Exit with note.
rule.rationale: IaC ceremony for these contexts wastes review time.
